summary: Review #97. Zero fixes since #96. Six items have now crossed into strike 5 territory — at this point the backlog isn't being triaged, it's being actively ignored. The codebase is visually polished and behaviourally rich, which makes the stagnant accessibility and code quality picture more frustrating. One genuinely new finding this cycle: the codebase uses two inconsistent touch-detection strategies in the same component tree — `(pointer: coarse)` at module scope in App.jsx and `(hover: none)` inline in Office.jsx Character click handler — producing subtly different behaviour on hybrid devices. Office.jsx has crept to 1676 lines. The normalize() function in syncFromGitHub silently defaults progress to 0 for all workers, which is the upstream root cause of the ProgressBar 0% display bug — fixing normalize() would fix the ProgressBar bug in one place.

suggestions.title: Worker cards tabIndex={-1} — strike 5, escalating to critical
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 324: `tabIndex={-1}` on the root WorkerCard div has now been flagged in five consecutive reviews with zero movement. This is not a minor polish issue — keyboard users cannot navigate the primary UI surface of this application. The card has `role='article'` and a perfect `aria-label` already in place; changing `tabIndex={-1}` to `tabIndex={0}` is a one-character edit. Add `.worker-card:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent); }` in App.css. This is now being escalated to critical: five reviews, two characters of change, still sitting untouched.

suggestions.title: ProgressBar renders unconditionally — root cause in normalize() — strike 2
suggestions.description: Two-part bug. (1) App.jsx syncFromGitHub normalize() at line 848 defaults `progress: typeof w.progress === 'number' ? w.progress : 0` for ALL workers, so idle workers always arrive with progress:0. (2) App.jsx line 380 renders `<ProgressBar progress={worker.progress} .../>` unconditionally. Fix both together: in normalize(), use `progress: typeof w.progress === 'number' ? w.progress : null`; in WorkerCard, guard with `{worker.progress != null && worker.status === 'working' && <ProgressBar ... />}`. Two-line change, immediately eliminates the PROGRESS 0% display on idle/error workers.

suggestions.title: Speech bubble escapes office floor container on mobile — strike 4
suggestions.description: Still confirmed from screenshots and unmodified in code: Bloberto's speech bubble uses `bottom: calc(100% + 8px)` in Office.css relative to the character element, placing it outside the floor's overflow boundary at small viewports. Bubble overlaps the tab navigation bar on mobile. Fix: clamp the bubble's vertical offset so it cannot escape the floor container, or conditionally flip the bubble to appear below the character when Bloberto occupies the top 25% of the floor. No code changes made since #93.

suggestions.title: NEW: Inconsistent touch detection — (pointer: coarse) vs (hover: none) in same tree
suggestions.description: The codebase uses two different media query strategies for touch detection with no coordination. App.jsx line 695: `isTouchDevice = window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').matches` (module-scope). Office.jsx line 748: `window.matchMedia('(hover: none)').matches` (evaluated per click). These queries do not always agree. On a Surface Pro with touchscreen: `(pointer: coarse)` is false (fine pointer active) but `(hover: none)` can be true. Result: the PAUSED pill shows 'Move mouse to refresh' but character click opens pinnedCard instead of navigating to dashboard — two behaviours that contradict each other on the same device. Fix: centralise touch detection into a single `useTouchDevice()` hook using `(hover: none)` (per pointer spec recommendation) and use it consistently in both App.jsx and Office.jsx.

suggestions.title: Vibe history pushed on initial mount — strike 5
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1116-1122: the vibe history push runs unconditionally on every render of the vibe-tracking effect, including the initial page load. `isInitialMount` is computed at line 1082 (`previousVibeKeyRef.current === null`) and correctly guards the streak counter at line 1083, but lines 1116-1122 are outside that guard. Every cold page load appends a duplicate entry to VIBE_HISTORY_KEY. After 12 loads the sparkline shows 12 identical 'in-flow' dots. Fix: wrap lines 1116-1122 with `if (!isInitialMount) { ... }`. Five reviews, four lines of change, still unguarded.

suggestions.title: ErrorBoundary fallback hardcoded dark-mode color — strike 5
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 17: `color: '#f87171'` is a hardcoded hex in the ErrorBoundary render method. In light mode this produces low-contrast red on near-white — fails WCAG 4.5:1 contrast ratio. The Retry button at line 19 has zero styling (no className, no padding). Fix: `color: 'var(--error, #f87171)'` and `className='btn'` on the Retry button. Five reviews, still untouched.

suggestions.title: isTouchDevice evaluated at module scope — stale on hybrid devices — strike 5
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 695: `const isTouchDevice = typeof window !== 'undefined' && window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').matches` is evaluated once at module import time. Hybrid device users who switch input modes mid-session see stale PAUSED pill copy. Additionally this is now an internal consistency issue — see the new '(pointer: coarse) vs (hover: none)' finding above. Fix: move into a `useTouchDevice()` hook (reuse the hook once written for the inconsistency fix above).

suggestions.title: Mobile pinged hover card has no visible dismiss control — strike 5
suggestions.description: Office.jsx lines 823-843: the char-hover-card rendered when `pinnedCard === true` contains name, role, task, and status — but no close button. On mobile the only dismiss path is tapping outside `.char`, an invisible affordance. The click-outside handler at lines 683-690 correctly clears `pinnedCard` on any outside click, but on a 375px viewport with densely packed characters, mis-taps are common. Fix: add `{pinnedCard && <button className='char-hover-card__close' onClick={(e) => { e.stopPropagation(); setPinnedCard(false) }} aria-label='Close card'>✕</button>}` as the first child of `.char-hover-card`. Five reviews, five lines of JSX + two lines of CSS, still absent.

suggestions.title: Footer quote button missing aria-label — strike 3
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1474: the footer button has `title='tap for another thought'` but no `aria-label`. The `title` attribute is not reliably announced by VoiceOver on iOS/Safari or NVDA on Windows. The button's visible text is a quote string with no semantic label for its action. Add `aria-label='Show another team quote'` (or dynamically: `aria-label={quoteBonus === 0 ? 'Show team quote' : 'Next quote (' + quoteBonus + ' of ' + BONUS_TAGLINES.length + ')'}`). Three reviews, one attribute, still missing.

suggestions.title: Character tooltips in CSS ::after pseudo-element — not in accessibility tree — strike 3
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 744-745: character tooltip text is set via `data-tooltip` attribute, rendered in CSS as `content: attr(data-tooltip)` on `::after` pseudo-element. CSS pseudo-element content is excluded from the accessibility tree. Workers' task descriptions and status details — the most information-dense data in the office view — are completely invisible to screen reader users. Fix: add `title={tooltipText}` alongside the existing `data-tooltip` (consistent with the desk monitor at Office.jsx ~line 1480 which already uses `title`), or use `aria-describedby` pointing to a visually-hidden span.

suggestions.title: footer-tagline button uses key prop to force DOM remount — anti-pattern — strike 2
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1472: `key={\`${teamVibe.key}-${quoteBonus}\`}` on a standalone `<button>` element (not in a list). React's key prop on a non-list element forces full unmount+remount on every vibe change or quote click. Side effects: focus loss, DOM churn, brief visual pop. Better: move the key to a wrapping `<span>` inside the button to remount only the quote text, or trigger the CSS entry animation via a className toggle instead.

suggestions.title: App.jsx 1482 lines, Office.jsx 1676 lines — both monolithic — strike 5
suggestions.description: App.jsx contains 13+ distinct components, all polling logic, all localStorage helpers, and the entire render tree. Office.jsx at 1676 lines has grown by 1 line since #96 and is now larger than App.jsx. No extraction has occurred. Minimum viable split: `components/WorkerCard.jsx`, `components/ActivityLog.jsx`, `components/StatsBar.jsx`, `hooks/useWorkerSync.js`, `hooks/useVibeTracking.js`. At 1676 lines, Office.jsx merge conflict probability per PR is approaching certainty. This is an ongoing reliability and onboarding cost, not just a style preference.

suggestions.title: Office window flat rectangle in dark mode — not fixed, visual regression persists
suggestions.description: WindowElement (Office.jsx line 848) now correctly renders a time-aware sky gradient — this was actually partially addressed in a previous cycle. However, at the time of review the element appears as a flat white/light rectangle in the qa97 screenshots (copied from #96). If this has been code-fixed but the screenshots pre-date it, disregard. If visible, add `border: 2px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.15)` as a window frame and confirm the gradient renders in dark mode.

suggestions.title: Stats bar may wrap to 2 rows at mid-range viewports — strike 3
suggestions.description: StatsBar can render up to 7 cards. At 900-1024px viewport width the flex-wrap layout creates an asymmetric 2-row layout with varying heights. The @media (max-width: 900px) breakpoint reduces font sizes but doesn't cap visible cards or introduce horizontal scroll. Consider `overflow-x: auto; scrollbar-width: none;` on the stats bar container for sub-1000px, or collapse error/stuck counts as icon badges on the primary stat cards at medium widths.

verified_fixes: No new regressions introduced — codebase is identical to #96 in all reviewed areas
WindowElement now has role='img' and aria-label describing the time-of-day sky — correct
Activity filter radiogroup has proper aria-label and keyboard navigation (ArrowLeft/Right) — working
Conference table SVG glow filter still uses useId() — no id collision risk
Full-sync and full-idle banners still have aria-live='polite'
No console.log or console.warn calls in production source — still clean
strike_five_hall_of_shame: Worker cards tabIndex={-1} — five consecutive reviews, one character of change needed
isTouchDevice module-scope constant — five consecutive reviews
ErrorBoundary hardcoded dark-mode color — five consecutive reviews, two lines of change needed
Vibe history pushed on initial mount — five consecutive reviews, four lines of change needed
App.jsx + Office.jsx monolithic files — five consecutive reviews, 3158 combined lines
Mobile pinged hover card no dismiss control — five consecutive reviews, five lines of JSX needed
praise: WindowElement (Office.jsx line 848) is genuinely impressive — time-aware sky gradients (sunrise/daylight/golden hour/night/midnight), animated clouds during daylight, stars at night, all behind a proper role='img' — this is the kind of detail that makes the office feel alive
The normalize() function in syncFromGitHub gracefully handles both flat-array and {workers:[], roster:[]} API formats — good defensive data handling

Character click handler correctly evaluates `window.matchMedia('(hover: none)')` at event time rather than at module scope — contrast with the stale isTouchDevice pattern, this shows the right instinct

WorkerCard flip animation (card front/back with aria-hidden toggling) is well-executed: focus management, tabIndex coordination between front and back panels, and the activity history layout are all clean
The `useMemo` discipline in Office.jsx (workingWorkers, idleWorkers, meetingWorkers, coffeeWorkers, standupWorkers all properly memoized at lines 1217-1225) remains the best part of the codebase
The idle-micro-bubble deterministic selection (line 813: hash-based index with no useState) is a clever trick that avoids flicker on re-render while still producing worker-specific emoji variation
